weather_report:
Perth settles into a quiet, crystalline night, the sky mostly open with just a few clouds wandering past the stars. It feels clean and unhurried out there, the kind of late hour that makes the streetlights seem gentler and the air a touch sharper. Through the small hours and toward dawn, the pattern holds: a largely clear sky, no real fuss, just a calm prelude to morning.

Once the sun lifts, expect a bright, mostly sunny day with only thin patches of cloud and good, honest visibility. The light should feel kind rather than fierce; the UV index will stay low early and build to a modest bite by midday, enough to justify sunglasses and a little sunscreen if you linger outdoors. It looks pleasantly crisp, cooler in the shade, so dress in easy layers—a light jacket to start, something you can shrug off when the sun warms the pavements. For now, a quiet cup by the window or a short stroll is just right; come morning, a gentle ride to the shops, a tidy in the garden, or reading in a sunny corner will match the day’s clear, unhurried mood.
